responsibilities: Design RF front-end circuits including amplifiers, mixers, and filters for wireless communication and radar systems.
Perform RF simulations using Keysight ADS or AWR Microwave Office to validate gain, noise figure, and linearity specifications.
Conduct antenna design and electromagnetic simulation using HFSS or CST Studio to optimize radiation patterns and impedance matching.
Characterize RF components and subsystems using vector network analyzers (VNA) and spectrum analyzers in lab environments.
Develop link budget analyses to determine system range, sensitivity, and performance under varying environmental conditions.
Debug RF hardware prototypes, resolving issues related to insertion loss, return loss, and signal distortion.
Collaborate with PCB layout engineers to ensure RF trace routing minimizes signal loss and electromagnetic interference.
Support EMI/EMC pre-compliance testing for RF subsystems to ensure regulatory certification readiness.
Design and validate RF power amplifier stages for efficiency, linearity, and thermal performance across operating bands.
Document RF design specifications, test reports, and calibration procedures for production and certification handoff.
